title: International Operation Begins to Rescue Sailors Trapped in Strait of Hormuz
subtitle: As a new peace agreement takes hold, the UN works to help thousands of stranded crew members while diplomatic tensions remain over the future of the vital waterway.

summary.en: The United Nations has launched a major operation to evacuate over 11,000 sailors trapped in the Strait of Hormuz. This effort follows a recent peace agreement between the United States and Iran to end their ongoing conflict. While shipping traffic has begun to increase, significant disagreements remain between the two nations. US officials continue to reject Iranian plans to charge tolls for using the international waterway. Meanwhile, regional stability remains fragile as diplomatic talks continue in several countries.
summary.zh: 聯合國已發起一項重大行動，撤離受困於荷莫茲海峽的超過一萬一千名船員。此舉是在美國與伊朗近期達成和平協議以結束持續衝突後所進行。儘管航運交通已開始增加，兩國之間仍存在重大分歧。美國官員持續拒絕伊朗對使用該國際水道收取通行費的計畫。與此同時，隨著多國外交談判持續進行，區域穩定性依然脆弱。

paragraphs.en: The United Nations’ International Maritime Organization (IMO) has officially started a large-scale operation to evacuate more than 11,000 sailors who have been stuck in the Strait of Hormuz. These workers have been unable to leave their vessels since February 28, when Iran effectively closed the waterway following the start of the war with Israel and the United States. According to the BBC, this mission is a key step toward restoring safety for civilian ships after months of difficulty for the crews involved.
paragraphs.zh: 聯合國旗下的國際海事組織（International Maritime Organization, IMO）已正式展開大規模行動，撤離受困於荷莫茲海峽的超過一萬一千名船員。自二月二十八日伊朗在與以色列及美國的戰爭爆發後實質封鎖該水道以來，這些工作人員便無法離開船隻。根據英國廣播公司（BBC）報導，這項任務是恢復民用船隻安全的重要一步，結束了相關船員長達數月的困境。

paragraphs.en: Arsenio Dominguez, the head of the IMO, stated that the operation is being carried out with the support of Iran, Oman, the United States, and other coastal nations. He noted that the organization has received the necessary safety guarantees to ensure the ships can move through the area without danger. According to Al Jazeera, this plan has been under discussion for several months and will be completed in stages to manage the risk of accidents in the busy waterway.
paragraphs.zh: 國際海事組織負責人多明格斯（Arsenio Dominguez）表示，這項行動是在伊朗、阿曼、美國及其他沿海國家的支持下進行。他指出，該組織已獲得必要的安全保證，確保船隻能安全通過該區域。根據半島電視台（Al Jazeera）報導，此計畫已討論數月，將分階段執行，以控管這條繁忙水道中發生意外的風險。

paragraphs.en: Since the signing of a peace agreement last week, shipping activity has started to rise. Data from the shipping intelligence agency Kpler showed that at least 36 commercial vessels passed through the strait on Monday, which is the highest number of ships seen since the conflict began. Despite this progress, the situation remains complicated. Al Jazeera reports that while talks between the US and Iran have improved, it is not yet clear how long it will take for the strait to return to normal operations.
paragraphs.zh: 自上週簽署和平協議以來，航運活動已開始回升。航運情報機構 Kpler 的數據顯示，週一至少有三十六艘商船通過該海峽，創下衝突開始以來的最高紀錄。儘管有所進展，局勢依然複雜。半島電視台報導稱，雖然美國與伊朗之間的對話有所改善，但海峽何時能恢復正常運作仍不明朗。

paragraphs.en: Even with the new peace deal, the United States and Iran continue to disagree on several important issues. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio arrived in the United Arab Emirates on Tuesday to discuss the future of the region. During his visit, he firmly stated that the Strait of Hormuz is an international waterway and that no country has the right to charge tolls or fees for ships passing through. According to the BBC, Rubio believes that other countries in the region agree with the American position that the strait must remain open and free for all.
paragraphs.zh: 即便有了新的和平協議，美國與伊朗在多項重要議題上仍存在分歧。美國國務卿盧比歐（Marco Rubio）週二抵達阿拉伯聯合大公國，商討該地區的未來。他在訪問期間堅定表示，荷莫茲海峽是國際水道，任何國家皆無權對通過的船隻徵收通行費或費用。根據 BBC 報導，盧比歐認為該地區其他國家也認同美國的立場，即海峽必須對所有人保持開放與自由。

paragraphs.en: In contrast, Iranian officials have suggested that they are looking into fees for maritime services. Tehran’s top negotiator, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, has insisted that the strait will not return to the way it was before the war. Furthermore, DW reports that Iran and Oman are currently exploring the costs of managing the waterway, with Iran continuing to push for a "maritime service fee." These conflicting claims highlight the ongoing tension between the two nations, even as they work to implement the terms of their recent agreement.
paragraphs.zh: 相對地，伊朗官員暗示他們正在研議收取海事服務費。德黑蘭當局的首席談判代表加利巴夫（Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf）堅持認為，海峽將不會回到戰前狀態。此外，德國之聲（DW）報導，伊朗與阿曼目前正在評估管理該水道的成本，而伊朗持續推動「海事服務費」的構想。這些相互矛盾的主張凸顯了兩國在致力落實近期協議條款的同時，緊張關係依然存在。

paragraphs.en: Beyond the issues at sea, the broader political situation remains sensitive. The US and Iran are still arguing over the details of their Memorandum of Understanding—a formal document that outlines their agreement. A major point of contention involves nuclear inspections. While President Donald Trump claimed on social media that Iran has agreed to high-level inspections by the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA), Iranian officials have stated that the agency will not be allowed to inspect nuclear sites that were bombed by the US and Israel last year. Additionally, Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian emphasized during a visit to Pakistan that Iran will not negotiate regarding its defensive capabilities, such as ballistic missiles.
paragraphs.zh: 除了海上議題，更廣泛的政治局勢依然敏感。美伊兩國仍在爭論諒解備忘錄（Memorandum of Understanding）的細節，這是一份概述雙方協議的正式文件。爭議的主要焦點在於核查工作。雖然川普（Donald Trump）總統在社群媒體上聲稱伊朗已同意接受國際原子能總署（International Atomic Energy Agency, IAEA）的高規格檢查，但伊朗官員表示，不會允許該機構檢查去年遭美以兩國轟炸的核設施。此外，伊朗總統裴澤斯基安（Masoud Pezeshkian）在訪問巴基斯坦期間強調，伊朗不會就彈道飛彈等防禦能力進行談判。

paragraphs.en: Domestic politics in the United States are also playing a role in these events. According to DW, the US Senate recently voted in favor of a resolution to limit the president’s power to take military action against Iran. While this measure is mostly symbolic and does not change the law, it shows that some members of the government are increasingly cautious about military involvement in the region. Meanwhile, in other parts of the Middle East, a separate, fragile ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah is facing challenges, as reports of violence in southern Lebanon continue to cause concern for international leaders.
paragraphs.zh: 美國國內政治也在這些事件中扮演角色。根據德國之聲報導，美國參議院近期投票通過一項決議，旨在限制總統對伊朗採取軍事行動的權力。雖然此舉多具象徵意義且不具法律約束力，但顯示出部分政府官員對該地區的軍事介入日益謹慎。與此同時，在中東其他地區，以色列與黎巴嫩真主黨之間另一份脆弱的停火協議正面臨挑戰，有關黎巴嫩南部暴力的報導持續引起國際領袖的擔憂。

paragraphs.en: As the evacuation of the 11,000 sailors continues, the world is watching to see if the peace agreement will hold. The IMO has promised to provide daily updates on the number of ships leaving the region safely. For now, the focus remains on the immediate safety of the seafarers who have spent months waiting for a way home. Whether the US and Iran can move past their disagreements over tolls and nuclear policy will determine the long-term stability of this critical global trade route.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著一萬一千名船員的撤離行動持續進行，全球都在關注和平協議是否能維持。國際海事組織承諾將每日更新安全離開該地區的船隻數量。目前，焦點仍放在這些等待回家已數月的船員之即時安全上。美國與伊朗能否克服在通行費與核政策上的分歧，將決定這條關鍵全球貿易路線的長期穩定性。

keyTerms.definitionEn: Unable to move or leave a particular place.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞 stick 的過去分詞，這裡作為形容詞描述狀態。文中：The United Nations’ International Maritime Organization (IMO) has officially started a large-scale operation to evacuate more than 11,000 sailors who have been stuck in the Strait of Hormuz.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Distinct periods or steps in a process or activity.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（上演），這裡作名詞表示過程中的不同時期。文中：According to Al Jazeera, this plan has been under discussion for several months and will be completed in stages to manage the risk of accidents in the busy waterway.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To remain effective, valid, or in force.
keyTerms.definitionZh: （協議、承諾等）持續有效、維持不變。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「拿、握」，這裡指協議是否能持續維持下去。文中：As the evacuation of the 11,000 sailors continues, the world is watching to see if the peace agreement will hold.

sources.name: Al Jazeera English
sources.title: UN starts evacuating 11,000 stranded sailors from Strait of Hormuz

sources.name: BBC News
sources.title: UN says it will evacuate sailors stranded in Strait of Hormuz, as Rubio warns against tolls

sources.name: Deutsche Welle (DW) Top
sources.title: US, Iran give conflicting claims over Hormuz fees

previousArticles.title: International Effort Begins to Rescue Sailors Trapped in Strait of Hormuz
previousArticles.summaryEn: The International Maritime Organization is launching a large-scale operation to evacuate 11,000 sailors stranded in the Gulf. This effort follows a recent ceasefire agreement between the United States and Iran. However, the two nations remain in disagreement over the details of nuclear inspections. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio has warned that no country can charge tolls on the Strait of Hormuz. Meanwhile, diplomatic discussions are ongoing to ensure the waterway remains open for international trade.

previousArticles.title: Tensions Rise as Iran Threatens to Close Vital Shipping Route
previousArticles.summaryEn: Iran has announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz in response to recent Israeli military strikes in Lebanon. This move threatens a fragile 60-day ceasefire agreement signed between the US and Iran just days ago. While Iran claims the waterway is closed, the US military insists that traffic is still moving normally. International leaders are now heading to Switzerland to continue peace talks despite the rising violence. The situation remains uncertain as both sides struggle to maintain the new peace deal.

quiz.question: According to the article, when were the 11,000 sailors first unable to leave their ships?

options.A: When the peace agreement was signed last week.
options.B: On February 28, when the war started and Iran closed the waterway.
options.C: When the IMO began its large-scale operation this week.
options.D: After the US Senate voted to limit the president's military power.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章第一段明確提到這些船員自 2 月 28 日（伊朗關閉水道時）起就無法離開船隻。選項 A 錯誤，因為和平協議是最近才簽署的；選項 C 錯誤，IMO 的行動是為了協助他們離開，而非導致受困的原因；選項 D 與船員受困的時間點無關。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the current relationship between the US and Iran regarding the Strait of Hormuz?

options.A: They have reached a complete consensus on how to manage the waterway.
options.B: They are no longer concerned about the strait because of the peace deal.
options.C: They still have significant disagreements, particularly regarding tolls and control.
options.D: They have agreed to let the IMO take full ownership of the strait.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第四段與第五段，美國主張水道應免費開放，而伊朗則堅持探討收取「海事服務費」的可能性，且伊朗官員表示水道不會回到戰前狀態。這顯示兩國在管理權與收費問題上仍有嚴重分歧，因此 A 與 B 皆錯誤。D 選項文中並未提及 IMO 將取得水道所有權。

quiz.question: In the sixth paragraph, what does the phrase 'a major point of contention' mean?

options.A: A topic that both sides agree on completely.
options.B: A subject that is causing disagreement or argument.
options.C: A minor detail that is not important to the negotiations.
options.D: A secret plan that has not been revealed to the public.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。Contention 的意思是「爭論」或「論點」，在文中指雙方對於核查問題持有不同意見，因此是導致爭執的焦點。選項 A 與文意相反；選項 C 錯誤，因為這是一個「major（主要）」的點，而非次要；選項 D 並非該詞的定義。

quiz.question: What is the primary focus of this article?

options.A: To explain the history of the Strait of Hormuz and its trade importance.
options.B: To detail the specific military strategies used by the US and Iran.
options.C: To report on the evacuation of sailors and the ongoing political instability despite a peace deal.
options.D: To argue that the IMO is the only organization capable of solving Middle East conflicts.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章開頭介紹了 IMO 對受困船員的撤離行動，隨後深入探討了儘管簽署了和平協議，美伊兩國在水道管理、核查及政治議題上仍存在持續的緊張與分歧。選項 A 僅是背景；選項 B 不是文章重點；選項 D 過度誇大了 IMO 的角色，文章並未主張它是唯一能解決衝突的單位。
